variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I've got thin docs for a full beginner guide—they cover barrier repair and mistake-avoidance, not a complete product list or dosing schedule. I'll build what you asked for using what's in your module plus foundational skincare logic. **core principle from your docs:** barrier health comes first. one active at a time. SPF is non-negotiable. ---     **key moves:**
1. **start with just cleanser** + moisturizer + SPF for 2 weeks. boring, but it lets you see your baseline and avoid barrier damage from day one.
2. **if you're acne-prone** azelaic acid AM is gentler than retinoids to start; adapalene PM is stronger but more irritating. pick one.
3. **if you're dry or** sensitive, skip actives entirely for the first 8 weeks. barrier repair (ceramides + panthenol) is your only "active."
4. **the moment you feel** burning, stinging, or flaking, pause the active. barrier damage spirals fast. - SPF is the only product that prevents aging, not reverses it. everything else is maintenance.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

steps.label: week 1–2: foundation
steps.detail: AM: cleanse + moisturizer + SPF. PM: cleanse + moisturizer. goal: establish routine, assess skin baseline. no actives yet.

steps.label: week 3–4: introduce one active
steps.detail: if acne-prone: add azelaic acid AM (daily) or adapalene PM (2x/week). if dry/sensitive: skip actives, focus on barrier repair with ceramides + panthenol.

steps.label: week 5–6: assess & adjust
steps.detail: if no irritation: keep active consistent. if stinging/redness: pause active, use only cleanser + moisturizer + SPF for 1 week. barrier comes first.

steps.label: week 7–8: consolidate
steps.detail: routine is stable. if acne-prone and tolerating adapalene 2x/week, can increase to 3x/week. if dry/sensitive, stay on barrier repair. reintroduce second active only after 8 weeks minimum.

items: buy: gentle cleanser, ceramide + panthenol moisturizer, SPF 30+ (mineral or chemical)
day 1: cleanse AM + PM, moisturize, apply SPF AM only. track any redness or stinging.
week 2: if skin is calm, pick ONE active (azelaic AM for acne, or skip if dry/sensitive).
week 3+: use active 2x/week if new. watch for barrier damage signs: burning, flaking, increased breakouts.
if irritation appears: pause active immediately, use only cleanser + moisturizer + SPF until calm (3–7 days).
week 8: assess. if tolerating active well, can increase frequency or add second active (not before 8 weeks).
never stack: no acids + retinoid + vitamin c at once. one active per routine.
SPF every AM, even indoors. non-negotiable.

cards.label: active per routine
cards.hint: stacking overwhelms skin and kills absorption

cards.label: minimum before adding a second active
starting frequency for retinoids
cards.hint: increase only after 4 weeks if no irritation

cards.label: SPF coverage required
cards.hint: UV damage is cumulative and irreversible
